name: Dandelion Wine (Easier)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick the dandelion flowers in sunshine or at midday when they are fully opened.
2. Measure two litres of flower heads, discarding as much green material as possible. Bring water to a boil simultaneously.
3. Pour the boiling water over the flower heads and cover with a tightly sealed container. Allow steeping for up to two days—avoid exceeding this time to prevent an undesirable odor that can spoil the wine.
4. Remove the flowers and stir in the sugar until dissolved.
5. Add yeast nutrient, orange juice, and yeast into the mixture. Transfer to a fermentation jar fitted with an airlock.
6. Bottle the wine once it clears, which should be ready for drinking within six months or sooner if desired.
